Home > Summer Internationals 2026

Summer Internationals 2026

The traditional summer rugby tours undertaken by the home unions will give way to the launch of the new 'Nations Championship' in 2026. The competition will consist of six rounds of matches plus a finals weekend, split across the Summer and Autumn Internationals.

Played every two years – in between Rugby World Cup and Lions Tour years – the tournament features two groups of teams competing in a Northern vs Southern Hemisphere format.

The Northern Hemisphere will be represented by England, France, Ireland, Italy, Scotland and Wales (the Six Nations teams), while the Southern Hemisphere group includes South Africa, New Zealand, Australia and Argentina (the SANZAAR nations), along with Fiji and Japan as invitational teams.

The 2026 edition is scheduled to begin on Saturday 4th July, with the opening three rounds to be played as part of the Summer Internationals.

Summer Internationals 2026 Fixtures

All kick-off times are shown in UK / Ireland time with local time shown in brackets (if applicable).

Round 1 | Round 2 | Round 3

Warm-up Matches

Friday 19th June 2026

France XV 35-19 England XV Stade de la Rabine, Vannes Attendance: 12,042

Saturday 27th June 2026

Barbarians 31-33 Wales Allianz Stadium, Twickenham Attendance: 20,000

ROUND 1

Saturday 4th July 2026

New Zealand 34-32 France One NZ Stadium, Christchurch Attendance: 29,152
Japan 27-10 Italy Chichibunomiya Rugby Stadium, Tokyo Attendance: 21,329
Australia 31-33 Ireland Allianz Stadium, Sydney Attendance: 41,971
Fiji 24-39 Wales Cardiff City Stadium, Cardiff Attendance: 16,456
South Africa 45-21 England Ellis Park, Johannesburg Attendance: 52,790
Argentina 38-47 Scotland Estadio Mario Alberto Kempes, Cordoba Attendance: 34,560

ROUND 2

Saturday 11th July 2026

New Zealand v Italy Hnry Stadium, Wellington Kick-off: 6:10am (5:10pm local)
Australia v France Suncorp Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 8:40am (5:40pm local)
Japan v Ireland McDonald Jones Stadium, Newcastle Kick-off: 11:10am (8:10pm local)
Fiji v England Hill Dickinson Stadium, Liverpool Kick-off: 2:10pm
South Africa v Scotland Loftus Versfeld Stadium, Pretoria Kick-off: 4:40pm (5:40pm local)
Argentina v Wales Estadio San Juan del Bicentenario, San Juan Kick-off: 8:10pm (4:10pm local)

ROUND 3

Saturday 18th July 2026

New Zealand v Ireland Eden Park, Auckland Kick-off: 8:10am (7:10pm local)
Japan v France Japan National Stadium, Tokyo Kick-off: 9:40am (5:40pm local)
Australia v Italy HBF Park, Perth Kick-off: 11:10am (6:10pm local)
Fiji v Scotland Scottish Gas Murrayfield, Edinburgh Kick-off: 2:10pm
South Africa v Wales Hollywoodbets Kings Park, Durban Kick-off: 4:40pm (5:40pm local)
Argentina v England Estadio Unico Madre de Ciudades, Santiago del Estero Kick-off: 8:10pm (4:10pm local)

For information about tickets and where to purchase them, please read our guide here: How to buy Summer International rugby tickets.

Subscribe for news and updates

Summer Internationals Rugby News

Finn Russell makes Scotland return against South Africa

9th July 2026

Finn Russell returns to the Scotland side as one of three changes to the starting XV for Saturday’s Nations Championship clash with South Africa in Pretoria.

Ireland hand debut to Sean Jansen in much-changed side to face Japan

9th July 2026

Sean Jansen will win his first cap in a much-changed Ireland side for Saturday’s Nations Championship clash with Japan in Australia.

Wales up for challenge of taking on Argentina's powerful scrum

8th July 2026

Wales coach Paul James says they are prepared to take on Argentina’s fearsome scrummaging unit in San Juan.

Gregor Townsend expects Finn Russell to return for Scotland's South Africa clash

7th July 2026

Gregor Townsend expects Finn Russell to return for Scotland in Saturday’s Nations Championship showdown with South Africa in Pretoria.

Emmanuel Iyogun and Chandler Cunningham-South called into England squad

7th July 2026

Emmanuel Iyogun and Chandler Cunningham-South have been called up to England’s squad for the Nations Championship match against Fiji at the Hill Dickinson Stadium on Saturday following more injury withdrawals.
